frameworks.name: temporal discounting
frameworks.one_line: future reward value discounted by delay drives duration categorization

frameworks.name: temporal bisection task
frameworks.one_line: classic short/long duration categorization paradigm reinterpreted with reward dynamics

frameworks.name: scalar timing / noisy duration estimation
frameworks.one_line: noisy internal duration estimates as one of two drivers of choice

frameworks.name: arithmetic vs geometric mean accounts of interval timing
frameworks.one_line: species-difference framing contrasting human arithmetic vs animal geometric bisection points

core_question: Why does the temporal bisection point sit near the geometric mean for rats but the arithmetic mean for humans?
key_assumptions: rats' choices reflect noisy duration estimates plus reward value
behavioral model can absorb species difference without separate timing mechanisms
manipulations: reward magnitude across short vs long options (unequal rewards)
task variant that eliminates temporally-discounted reward component
key_findings: model predicts bisection-point shifts induced by unequal rewards
model accounts for previously paradoxical psychometric reversals
removing the temporal-discounting component shifts rats' bisection point from geometric to arithmetic mean
interpretations: apparent human/non-human difference in interval timing is largely a reward-discounting artefact, not a representational divergence
rats' bisection behavior is jointly driven by duration noise and discounted reward value
